summary: This page concludes an example of time expansion, showing how replacing t by t/2 stretches a signal in time and shifts key time instants accordingly. It then introduces time inversion (time reversal), explaining that reflecting a signal about the vertical axis produces the signal f(-t), illustrated by Fig. 1.12 and formalized in equation (1.19).

figures.caption: Two plots demonstrating time inversion of a signal. Plot (a) shows the original signal f(t). Plot (b) shows the time-inverted signal φ(t) = f(-t), which is a reflection of f(t) about the vertical axis.
